title: TRAINEE SHIPBROKER, GEARED DESK
description:
Job Description & Requirements

IFCHOR GALBRAITHS is a globally recognised market leading brokerage, with over20 offices and 350 employees globally. Aside from our reputation as competitive brokers, IG delivers exceptional market insights through our Research Division, outstanding market access to clients to Carbon Solutions through our Sustainability offering and an innovative platform to our brokers through the continued development digital solutions for our desks, delivered by our in-house team.

As part of our continued expansion, we’re looking for experienced shipbrokers to join our Geared desk in Singapore.

About the Role:

You will be working on key company accounts as well as developing your own client base with the support from your colleagues globally. As a broker, you will be bringing owners and charterers together, negotiating charterparties relating specifically to Handy-Supramax/Ultramax tonnage.

You will be supported by our in-house post fixture operations team with 24-hour coverage on all fixtures. You will be working within a highly effective and high-performing team with access to global insights and information to help you better support your clients.

Experience & Requirements:

2+ years’ experience in a similar role. Established network with small-medium sized traders. Proven track record completing competitive business. Proficient and effective communication skills with fluent English being essential.

Team-minded, proactive about business development and keen to work within a multinational, multicultural environment. Existing Working Entitlement for Singapore.

postedCompany.description:
SHIPBROKING AT IFCHOR GROUP (SINGAPORE)

Shipbroking at IFCHOR Group (Singapore) a top-tier international shipbroking group active in the dry bulk and tanker markets is looking for an intern to support its shipbroking activities in Singapore. Our Singapore based team addresses the daily chartering needs of major commodity trading houses, shipping operators, mining companies and shipping companies. Should be prepared to work in a fast-paced team environment, and will finish the internship having gained broad experience in Panamax size shipbroking practices

 

Roles & Responsibilities

• Market tonnage to customers/ shippers/ charterers

• Negotiate spot/time charters to achieve maximum income for the owners

• Check through charter parties

• Be aware of the positions and desired strategic directions for each ship assigned

• Coordinate the allocation of sport and contract cargo to the ship assigned to the specific service

• Set up and negotiate deals, including presenting the business to potential clients, negotiating the main terms of a contract or sale and seeing it through to its conclusion and any follow up that may be involved

• Coordinate with chartering and operations departments to maximize space utilization, minimize the number of port and berth calls and maximize the voyage’s financial results

• Coordinate all aspects of trans-shipments for respective cargoes

• Maintain current voyage records and documentation to customers for tonnage loading within the service

• Maintain a thorough knowledge of all relevant charter party forms and company standard terms Requirements

• Strong commercial sense and ability to think on your feet

• Client management/ relationship building skills

• Ability to negotiate is vital as this is a principle skill requirement in Ship Brokering

• Good understanding of accounting and numeracy skills are essential

• Excellent presentation skills and the ability to communicate well
postedCompany.name: IFCHOR GALBRAITHS (SINGAPORE) PTE. LTD.

districts.location: D01 Cecil, Marina, People’s Park, Raffles Place
